chapter: Chapter: HOW THE STATE GOVERNMENT WORKS

questions.question: What is the full form of MLA?
questions.answer: Member of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: How are MLAs elected?
questions.answer: They are directly elected by the people of a constituency.

questions.question: What is a 'Constituency'?
questions.answer: A particular area from which all the voters living there choose one representative.

questions.question: Which party is called the 'Majority party'?
questions.answer: The political party that has won more than half the number of constituencies in a state.

questions.question: What is the 'Ruling Party'?
questions.answer: The political party that has the majority and forms the government.

questions.question: Who are the 'Opposition'?
questions.answer: All the elected representatives who are not members of the ruling party.

questions.question: Who is the head of the State?
questions.answer: The Governor.

questions.question: Who appoints the Governor of a state?
questions.answer: The Central Government.

questions.question: Who chooses the Chief Minister?
questions.answer: The MLAs of the ruling party choose their leader who becomes the Chief Minister.

questions.question: What is the 'Legislative Assembly'?
questions.answer: A place where all the MLAs, whether from the ruling party or the opposition, meet to discuss various things.

questions.question: Who has the dual responsibility as an MLA and a Minister?
questions.answer: The MLAs who are appointed as ministers by the Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister.

questions.question: What is a 'Press Conference'?
questions.answer: A gathering of journalists who are invited to hear about and ask questions on a particular issue.

questions.question: What does the government do to address problems in the Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: It answers questions, debates issues, and passes laws.

questions.question: What is the main problem discussed in the story of Patalpuram?
questions.answer: Shortage of clean drinking water and the spread of diarrhoea.

questions.question: Can an MLA belong to no political party?
questions.answer: Yes, such candidates are called 'Independent' candidates.

questions.question: What is the role of the Cabinet?
questions.answer: It is a group of senior ministers who take important decisions for the state.

questions.question: Where are laws for the entire country made?
questions.answer: In the Parliament.

questions.question: How does the public express its opinion about government work?
questions.answer: Through newspapers, TV channels, protests, and rallies.

questions.question: Who is responsible for the overall working of the state government?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister and other ministers.

questions.question: What is the executive part of the government?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister and the ministers who head various departments.

questions.question: What is the legislative part of the government?
questions.answer: All the MLAs who assemble in the legislative assembly.

questions.question: What is a 'Coalition'?
questions.answer: When no single party gets a majority and two or more parties come together to form a government.

questions.question: Name any one government department.
questions.answer: The Health Department (or Education/Agriculture department).

questions.question: Who must approve the laws made in the Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: The Governor.

questions.question: Do all states in India have a Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: Yes, every state in India has a Legislative Assembly.
